Based on the search results, here's what I found about React Native's latest stable version in 2026:

## Current Latest Version

The latest stable version is 0.85.2, last published 8 days ago. However, there are references to more recent versions in the documentation and articles.

## Notable Versions in 2026

React Native 0.84 introduces Hermes V1 as the default JS engine, the Fabric renderer, and TurboModules. The New Architecture is the default since 0.76, the old bridge was retired in 0.82, and Hermes V1 shipped as the default JavaScript engine in 0.84.

Additionally, React Native 0.83 is a stable release with no user-facing breaking changes.

## Recommendation

For new projects, the most recent stable version will be used automatically whenever a new project is created using the npx react-native init command.

Here's what I found about Together AI's free tier in 2026:

## Free Credits and Sign-up

Together AI gives new users $25 in free credits to experiment with 200+ open-source models, including serverless inference and fine-tuning. However, more recent 2026 information indicates this has evolved: Together AI gives up to $100 in free credits at sign-up, one of the largest trial credit amounts in the industry.

## Startup Accelerator Program

Beyond basic free credits, Together AI provides access to their Startup Accelerator program offering $15,000 to $50,000 based on your company stage and profile.

## Free Models

Together AI currently offers 70 models at no cost, which includes popular open-source models like various versions of Llama, Qwen, Gemma, and DeepSeek models.

## Premium Tier Access

Some premium models (Flux Pro, dedicated endpoints) require Build Tier 2+, which unlocks after $5 in actual spend.

## General Pricing Model

Their model is largely pay-as-you-go, with options for token or GPU usage for their serverless offerings.

Together AI's free tier stands out as one of the most generous among AI platforms in 2026, particularly for developers interested in working with open-source models.

Links: [{"title":"create-expo-app - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/more/create-expo/"},{"title":"Create a project - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/get-started/create-a-project/"},{"title":"Create your first app - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/tutorial/create-your-first-app/"},{"title":"Develop an app with Expo - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/workflow/overview/"},{"title":"Expo CLI - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/more/expo-cli/"},{"title":"Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/"},{"title":"create-expo-app - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-expo-app"},{"title":"How to create expo app: Start to Finish - CatDoes","url":"https://catdoes.com/blog/create-expo-app"},{"title":"Create Expo App","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-expo"},{"title":"How to Create an Expo App: The Founder's Guide to Building Faster | RapidNative","url":"https://www.rapidnative.com/blogs/create-expo-app"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about Expo project initialization commands using `npx create-expo-app`:

## Creating an Expo Project

create-expo-app is a command-line tool to create and set up a new Expo and React Native project, simplifying the initialization process by providing various templates to get started quickly without the need for manual configuration.

## Basic Command

All you really need is Node.js installed, and from there, a single command, `npx create-expo-app YourAppName`, scaffolds a complete React Native project for you.

## Starting the Development Server

Once that's done, you just run `npx expo start` to fire up the development server.

## Using Templates

Create Expo App prepares an empty Expo project by default. You can choose to use a project with more prepared functionality by starting Create Expo App with the --template flag. For example: `npx create-expo --template tabs` or `npx create-expo --template expo-template-tabs`

## Important SDK Note

During the SDK 55 transition period, create-expo-app@latest without the --template flag creates an SDK 54 project. If you plan to use Expo Go on a physical device, use an SDK 54 project. Otherwise, use --template default@sdk-55 to create an SDK 55 project.

## Additional Commands

Start a server for developing your app with `npx expo start`. Generate the native Android and iOS directories for your project with `npx expo prebuild`. Build and run the native apps locally with `npx expo run:ios` and `npx expo run:android`.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Hugging Face Inference API free tier in 2026:

## Free Tier Overview

Hugging Face Inference Providers includes a generous free tier, with additional credits for PRO users and Team & Enterprise organizations. Hugging Face provides a Serverless Inference API as a way for users to quickly test and evaluate thousands of publicly accessible machine learning models with simple API calls for free.

## Free Tier Limitations

There are rate limits for regular Hugging Face users (~few hundred requests per hour). Additionally, past the free-tier credits, you get charged for every inference request based on the compute time x price of the underlying hardware.

## Upgrade Options

For access to higher rate limits, you can upgrade to a PRO account for just $9 per month. The PRO plan includes 8x increased ZeroGPU quota with up to 25 minutes of H200 compute daily, 1TB of private storage (10x the free tier), 10TB public storage, 2M monthly Inference Provider credits, and up to 10 ZeroGPU Spaces with Dev Mode.

The free tier is designed primarily for experimentation and testing rather than production workloads, while paid tiers offer significantly higher usage allowances and additional features.

Here's a summary of the search results for Expo Supabase Firebase integration and free tier information in 2026:

## Free Tier Comparison

**Supabase Free Tier:**
The Free plan includes 2 projects with 500 MB database storage and 50,000 monthly active users. Supabase's free tier allows unlimited API requests with no daily caps, making it significantly more practical for prototyping and early-stage development.

**Firebase Free Tier:**
Firebase's free tier is more generous for storage (5GB vs 1GB), but its daily read/write limits (50K reads, 20K writes, 20K deletes) can throttle even modest development workflows.

## Key Differences

**Pricing Model:**
Supabase uses predictable, tier-based pricing with generous included resources. Firebase uses a pay-as-you-go model where costs scale with every read, write, and delete operation.

**Technology:**
Supabase lets you leverage relational database expertise directly. No need to learn Firestore's query language, security rules syntax, or NoSQL data modeling patterns.

**Best Use Cases:**
If you are building a mobile application and need authentication, push notifications, analytics, crash reporting, remote configuration, and A/B testing from a single SDK, Firebase is unmatched. No other BaaS platform provides this level of integrated mobile tooling.

The search results mention Expo as a tool for React Native development but don't contain specific information about Expo-Supabase-Firebase integration patterns.

Free Tier Limits & Upgrade Triggers (2026)","url":"https://costbench.com/software/llm-api-providers/groq/free-plan/"},{"title":"Groq Cloud Pricing and Free Tier Details 2026 — NeuraPulse","url":"https://neuraplus-ai.github.io/blog/groq-cloud-pricing-free-tier.html"}]

## Groq Free Tier API 2026

Groq offers a free tier accessible by signing up at console.groq.com with no credit card required. Here are the key details about the free tier in 2026:

### Rate Limits
The standard free tier includes 30 requests per minute, 6,000 tokens per minute, and 14,400 requests per day per model. Rate limits apply at the organization level, not individual users.

### What's Included
The free tier lets you use GroqCloud and call supported models, with your usage capped by rate limits. Available models on the free tier include Llama 3.1 8B, Llama 3.3 70B, and other open-source models.

### Best Use Cases
The free tier is designed for prototyping and demos, allowing you to spin up a proof of concept, validate the idea, then decide whether to upgrade.

### Upgrading Options
The Developer tier (which requires a credit card but no minimum spend) unlocks 10x rate limits and 25% cheaper tokens. The Batch API offers efficient parallel processing for high-volume workloads at a 25% cost discount compared to synchronous APIs.

Links: [{"title":"Build APKs for Android Emulators and devices - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/build-reference/apk/"},{"title":"EAS Build - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/build/introduction/"},{"title":"Create your first build - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/build/setup/"},{"title":"Building an APK from Expo: Everything You Need to Know! - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/khokon/building-an-apk-from-expo-everything-you-need-to-know-c7i"},{"title":"Create a development build on EAS - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/develop/development-builds/create-a-build/"},{"title":"Create and run a cloud build for Android - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/tutorial/eas/android-development-build/"},{"title":"Create a production build for Android - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/tutorial/eas/android-production-build/"},{"title":"Building APKs locally with expo: a step-by-step guide","url":"https://www.qed42.com/insights/building-apks-locally-with-expo-a-step-by-step-guide"},{"title":"Build your project for app stores - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/deploy/build-project/"},{"title":"Android build process - Expo Documentation","url":"https://docs.expo.dev/build-reference/android-builds/"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about Expo build APK commands using EAS Build:

## Key Commands for Building APKs with EAS Build

The basic command to build is `eas build`, which sends your project to EAS Build and produces installable binaries for Android and iOS. You can also build for one platform at a time by passing in --platform android or --platform ios as desired.

## Building APK Files Specifically

To generate an .apk, modify the eas.json by adding one of the following properties in a build profile: android.gradleCommand to :app:assembleRelease, :app:assembleDebug, :app:assembleDebugOptimized (available for SDK 54 and later), or any other Gradle command.

You can run: `eas build --profile development --platform android` - make sure you're logged into your Expo account before running this command.

## Configuration in eas.json

To get an APK instead of an AAB, you'll need to modify your eas.json file to include: `{ "build": { "production": { "autoIncrement": true, "android": { "buildType": "apk" } } } }`

## Building for Android Emulator/Device

For Android, the development build must be in the .apk format. While the default Android format is .aab, which is ideal for Google Play Store distribution, it cannot be installed on devices or emulators.

## After the Build Completes

Once your build is completed, download the APK from the build details page or the link provided when eas build is done. Run adb install path/to/the/file.apk.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Ollama, free LLM APIs, and local LLM solutions in 2026:

## What is Ollama?

Ollama is the easiest way to automate your work using open models, while keeping your data safe. Ollama is an open-source LLM platform that brings powerful language models to your machine with zero infrastructure overhead.

## Key Features

Ollama has a REST API for running and managing models. Ollama is developer-focused with a CLI-first approach, a REST API, and Docker support — ideal for integration into applications and pipelines.

Unlike cloud-based LLM platforms that charge per token, Ollama is completely free and MIT-licensed. OpenAI-Compatible API: Drop-in replacement for OpenAI endpoints; any app built for ChatGPT works with Ollama with a single URL change.

## 2026 Updates & Capabilities

The 2026 updates (multimodal support, Q4_K_M quantization, web search integration) make it production-ready for a broader range of use cases.

Ollama can deliver 300+ tokens per second on consumer hardware with GPU acceleration, and up to 1,200 tokens per second on high-end setups.

## Popular Models Available

Top picks include: Kimi K2.6 for coding, Kimi K2.6 for best overall quality, Kimi K2.5 (Reasoning) for fast responses, DeepSeek V4 Pro for long documents, and DeepSeek V4 Flash for cheap API access.

## When to Use Ollama

Use Ollama when: data privacy is non-negotiable, you need offline capability, your use case involves high-volume inference that would be cost-prohibitive with APIs (processing thousands of documents), you are prototyping and iterating rapidly on prompts, or you want to fine-tune and customize models.

content.text:
Based on my research, here's a comprehensive guide on the latest Expo and React Native versions, key commands, and free service integrations as of early 2026:

---

## Latest Stable Versions (April 2026)

**Expo SDK:** Version 55.0.15 (released February 25, 2026)
- Includes React Native 0.83
- Features: Expo Go UI revamp, Hermes v1 support, enhanced AI tooling
- Note: During SDK 55 transition, `create-expo-app@latest` creates SDK 54 projects by default. Use `--template default@sdk-55` for SDK 55.

**React Native:** Version 0.85.2 (latest stable, published 8 days ago as of late April 2026)
- Also compatible with 0.84 and 0.83
- New Architecture is now the default since 0.76
- Hermes V1 is the default JavaScript engine since 0.84
- The old bridge was retired in 0.82

---

## Key Commands

### Initialize an Expo Project
```bash
npx create-expo-app YourAppName
npx expo start  # Start development server
```

For SDK 55 specifically:
```bash
npx create-expo --template default@sdk-55
```

For template-based projects:
```bash
npx create-expo --template tabs
```

### Build APKs with EAS Build
```bash
eas build --platform android
eas build --profile development --platform android  # Development build
```

To generate .apk (instead of .aab):
Modify `eas.json`:
```json
{
  "build": {
    "production": {
      "autoIncrement": true,
      "android": {
        "buildType": "apk"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Local Native Builds
```bash
npx expo prebuild  # Generate native Android/iOS directories
npx expo run:android
npx expo run:ios
```

---

## Free Backend Services Integration

### Supabase
- **Free Tier:** 2 projects, 500MB storage, 50,000 monthly active users
- **Advantages:** Unlimited API requests (no daily caps), PostgreSQL-based, predictable pricing
- **Best for:** Early-stage development, relational database needs

### Firebase
- **Free Tier:** 5GB storage, 50K reads/day, 20K writes/day, 20K deletes/day
- **Advantages:** Integrated mobile tooling, authentication, push notifications, analytics, crash reporting
- **Trade-offs:** Pay-as-you-go pricing can throttle development workflows
- **Best for:** Full mobile app ecosystem (auth, analytics, A/B testing)

**Key Difference:** Supabase favors predictable tier-based pricing with generous resources; Firebase offers integrated mobile tools but uses per-operation pricing.

---

## Free/Open-Source LLM APIs

### Groq
- **Free Tier:** No credit card required, sign up at console.groq.com
- **Rate Limits:** 30 requests/min, 6,000 tokens/min, 14,400 requests/day
- **Available Models:** Llama 3.1 8B, Llama 3.3 70B, and others
- **Upgrade:** Developer tier (10x rate limits, 25% cheaper tokens) after credit card entry
- **Best for:** Prototyping and demos

### Together AI
- **Free Credits:** Up to $100 at sign-up (among highest in the industry)
- **Free Models:** 70+ open-source models available (Llama, Qwen, Gemma, DeepSeek variants)
- **Startup Program:** $15,000-$50,000 for startups
- **Premium Models:** Require $5+ actual spend (Build Tier 2+)
- **Pricing:** Pay-as-you-go for tokens

### Hugging Face Inference API
- **Free Tier:** No credit card needed, rate-limited (~few hundred requests/hour)
- **Features:** Access to 1000s of publicly accessible ML models via simple API calls
- **Upgrade:** PRO ($9/month) offers 8x ZeroGPU quota, 1TB private storage, 2M monthly credits
- **Best for:** Experimentation and testing, not production workloads

### Ollama (Self-Hosted)
- **Cost:** Completely free and MIT-licensed
- **Setup:** Run LLMs locally with zero infrastructure overhead
- **API:** REST API with OpenAI-compatible endpoints (drop-in ChatGPT replacement)
- **Performance:** 300+ tokens/sec on consumer hardware with GPU, 1,200+ on high-end
- **Available Models:** 300+ models including Kimi K2.5/K2.6, DeepSeek V4, and others
- **Best for:** Data privacy, offline capability, high-volume inference, fine-tuning

**Data Privacy Note:** Ollama is the only fully offline option; all cloud providers transmit data to their servers.

---

## Summary Table: Free LLM Services

| Provider | Free Credits | Rate Limits | Best Use Case | Privacy |
|----------|-------------|------------|---------------|---------|
| Groq | No | 30 RPM, 6K TPM | Prototyping | Cloud |
| Together AI | Up to $100 | 70+ free models | Production testing | Cloud |
| Hugging Face | Limited | ~Few hundred/hr | Experimentation | Cloud |
| Ollama | N/A (self-hosted) | Unlimited locally | Privacy-first apps | Local |
